rulings.Case Title: HAZEL HAASE ET AL VS. RILEY POWER INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025 in Department 304, Line 1. Intervenors Transcontinental Company, Industrial Indemnity Company, and Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, Amended Motion to Leave to Intervene on Behalf of Enterprise Plumbing, Inc., is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 387(d)(2). Opposition filed by Plaintiffs. Reply filed. The Court GRANTS Intervenors' Request for Judicial Notice. The Court exercises its discretion to permit Intervenors to intervene. Intervenors represent they are the insurers for Enterprise Plumbing, Inc., a terminated California corporation that lacks the ability to defend itself. (See Thacker Decl. 5 ["If Enterprise Plumbing, Inc. were an active corporation, Intervenors would provide a defense under a reservation of rights under their respective insurance policies."].) Since Intervenors would be liable for any ensuing default judgment, they have a direct interest in this litigation and their interests will be prejudiced unless they are allowed to intervene. (See Ins. Code 11580(b)(2); Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196, 1204-1205, citing Reliance Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 383, 385-388 [insurer may intervene where it is liable for default judgment]; Kaufman & Broad Communities, Inc. v. Performance Plastering, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 212, 216 [insurer must intervene to protect its rights where its insured is a suspended corporation defendant].) Intervenors' admittedly sparse showing is sufficient; at this stage, Intervenors need not identify or attach a copy of the applicable policy or policies, which Plaintiffs may obtain through discovery, nor show that they do not contain asbestos exclusions. Thus, the Court finds good cause to allow the intervention. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on January 14, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (304/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT HOUSTON MUCKELVANE VS. AMCORD, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Motion And Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025 in Department 304, Line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. 473(a)(1). No opposition filed. Plaintiff shall file and serve the First Amended Complaint forthwith on the newly added defendant Union Carbide Corporation, (previously sued as Doe 65 and 251), as well as on those defendants who have not yet appeared in the case. It is further ordered that existing Defendants (i) Amcord, Inc.; (ii) Omega Products Corp.; and (iii) A & A Building Material Co., have been added as parties to the First through Fourth Causes of Action of the proposed First Amended Complaint. It is further ordered that (i) Tierra Chesney; (ii) Traci Muckelvane; (iii) Todd Muckelvane; and (iv) Travis Muckelvance, are to be added as wrongful death plaintiffs in this action. Lastly, it is ordered that Trevor Muckelvane is added as a Defendant Heir in this action. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all other Defendants who have previously appeared in the action. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on January 14, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (304/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNY STEVENSON VS. RILEY POWER INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025 in Department 304, Line 3. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). 1. The trial date is May 5, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206. a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is May 14, 2025. b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20. 2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 29, 2025. a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation. c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing. 3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days. a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter. 4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. 5. The fact discovery cut-off date is April 18, 2025. 6. The expert discovery cut-off date is May 2, 2025. 7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 13, 2025. a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission. b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Tentative ruling continued, see next entry)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ISSUANCE OF TENTATIVE RULING ONLY)
rulings.Rulings: (PART 2 OF 2) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry) 8. No later than January 28, 2025: a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant. b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses. c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. 9. A further case management conference is set for March 18, 2025, at 4:00 p.m. a. Case management conference statements are not required. b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom. c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing. 10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than March 31, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due April 14, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on April 21, 2025. 11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, May 1, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference. The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest.
